title: Begumpul to Delhi in 55 Minutes: A First-Timer's Complete Guide to the RRTS Corridor
tags: Begumpul to Delhi, Begumpul to Delhi RRTS, Begumpul to Delhi metro route, Begumpul to Delhi metro ticket price, Begumpul to Delhi distance, Begumpul metro station to Delhi, Delhi to Begumpul Meerut route, Begumpul to Delhi metro route timings, Namo Bharat Begumpul, RRTS Meerut to Delhi
description: Travel from Begumpul to Delhi in about 55 minutes on the Namo Bharat RRTS. Complete guide covering route, stations, ticket price, distance, timings, and first-ride tips for daily commuters.
body:
If you live near [Begumpul ](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/begumpul.html)in Meerut and want to reach Delhi fast, you are in the right place. Begumpul is one of the major stations on the Delhi–Meerut RRTS corridor, also known as the Namo Bharat train system. This route connects Meerut with Delhi quickly, smoothly, and at a very affordable cost.

This guide is written for first-time riders and daily commuters who want a simple, honest, and complete answer about the route, the stations, the ticket price, the travel time, and what to expect on the ride. You will not need to read anything else after this.

Whether you searched for "Begumpul to Delhi metro route," "Begumpul to Delhi RRTS ticket price," "Begumpul to Delhi distance," or simply "how to go from Begumpul to Delhi fast," this blog answers all of it in plain language.

## **Why This Route Matters for Daily Commuters**

Road travel between Meerut and Delhi is painful. Traffic on NH-58 and NH-34 can stretch a 60-kilometre journey into a 2.5 to 3-hour crawl. Add parking stress, rising fuel costs, and the physical exhaustion of driving or sitting in a bus, and you understand why so many people are looking for a better option.

The RRTS corridor changes completely. It gives Meerut residents a fast, predictable, air-conditioned train service that runs at speeds up to 160 km/h. For office workers, students, business travellers, and anyone who makes the Meerut-Delhi trip regularly, this is not just convenient. It is genuinely life-changing.

Here is what this route solves for most commuters:

- It cuts travel time from 2 to 3 hours by road down to under an hour by train.
- It removes the stress of traffic jams, road accidents, and unpredictable travel time.
- It costs far less than fuel and tolls for private vehicles.
- It gives you a fixed schedule you can plan your day around.
- It is comfortable enough to read, work, or rest during the commute.

If you travel between Meerut and Delhi more than two or three times a week, this route will save you money, time, and energy every single month.

## **Begumpul to Delhi — Quick Summary at a Glance**

Before going into detail, here is everything important in one place:

| **Detail** | **Information** |
| Travel mode | RRTS / Namo Bharat |
| Starting station | Begumpul (Meerut) |
| Main Delhi destination | Sarai Kale Khan |
| Approximate travel time | 55 to 58 minutes (full corridor) |
| Full corridor length | 82.15 km |
| Maximum train speed | 160 km/h |
| Ticket type | Standard and Premium class available |
| Fare range | Starting from approximately Rs. 20 (distance-based) |
| Ticketing options | App, QR code, station machine, contactless card |
| Service style | Fast, frequent, air-conditioned commuter trains |

This is your quick reference. Everything below explains each of these points in full detail.

## **What Is the RRTS and How Is It Different from the Delhi Metro?**

Many people search "Begumpul to Delhi metro" because they assume this is a metro line. It is not. The RRTS (Rapid Rail Transit System) and the Delhi Metro are two separate systems.

Here is the simple difference:

**Delhi Metro** runs within Delhi and a few neighbouring areas. It is designed for short trips inside the city. Stations are close together, and trains stop frequently.

**RRTS (Namo Bharat)** is a high-speed intercity rail system. It connects Meerut with Delhi over a long distance at a much higher speed. Trains stop less frequently because the focus is on covering distance fast, not on short city hops.

So when you are travelling from Begumpul in Meerut to Delhi, you are using the RRTS, not the metro. The two systems are different in design, speed, fare structure, and purpose. However, once you reach Delhi-side stations like Sarai Kale Khan or Anand Vihar, you can connect to the Delhi Metro for further travel within the city.

Think of it this way: RRTS gets you from Meerut to Delhi quickly. The Delhi Metro then takes you to your final destination inside Delhi.

## **The Full Route: Begumpul to Delhi Step by Step**

The Delhi–Meerut RRTS corridor stretches 82.15 km from Sarai Kale Khan in Delhi to Modipuram in Meerut. Begumpul is one of the main stations on the Meerut end of this corridor.

When you board at Begumpul, the train travels through Meerut, passes through Ghaziabad, and enters Delhi on the other end. Here is the full list of stations along the corridor in order, starting from Meerut and going toward Delhi:

**Meerut (City) Stations:**

- [Modipuram](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/modipuram.html)
- Shatabdi Nagar
- Begumpul ← _your starting point_
- Meerut Central
- [Bhaisali](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/bhaisali-bus-adda.html)
- Meerut South

**Ghaziabad and Corridor Stations:**

- Modinagar North
- Modinagar South
- Muradnagar
- Duhai
- Guldhar
- Ghaziabad

**Delhi and Near-Delhi Stations:**

- [Sahibabad](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/sahibabad.html)
- [Anand Vihar](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/anand-vihar.html)
- [New Ashok Nagar](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/new-ashok-nagar.html)
- [Sarai Kale Khan](https://metroguideindia.com/namo-bharat/stations/sarai-kale-khan.html) ← _main destination in Delhi_

When you travel from Begumpul toward Delhi, your train moves through this sequence. You do not need to change trains for the full journey. One train takes you all the way from Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan.

## **Where Is Begumpul Station?**

Begumpul station is located in central Meerut. It is one of the most accessible RRTS stations for people living in the core Meerut city area. If you live near Begumpul Chowk, Hapur Road, Abu Lane, or surrounding localities, this is your nearest RRTS station.

The station is elevated, which means you walk up to the platform via escalators or stairs. It follows the standard RRTS station design — clean, organised, with security checks at entry, ticketing machines and counters available, digital displays showing train timings, and seating areas on the platform.

For many Meerut residents, Begumpul is more convenient than Meerut Central or other stations because of where it sits in the city. If this is your closest station, you can walk to it, take an auto or e-rickshaw, or park a two-wheeler nearby.

## **How Much Time Does Begumpul to Delhi Take?**

This is the most searched question, so let us answer it very clearly.

The full RRTS corridor from Modipuram (northernmost Meerut station) to Sarai Kale Khan (Delhi) is 82.15 km. The full end-to-end journey takes approximately 55 to 58 minutes when all stations are counted.

Since Begumpul is not the first station — it sits several stations into the Meerut section — your journey from Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan will be slightly shorter than the full corridor time.

A rough estimate for Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan is approximately 45 to 55 minutes, depending on whether you are taking a standard stopping service or an express-style run that skips some stations.

Trains on this corridor travel at up to 160 km/h, making this one of the fastest rail commutes available in the Delhi-NCR region.

To compare:

| **Route** | **Mode** | **Approximate Time** |
| Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan | RRTS | \~45 to 55 minutes |
| Meerut to Delhi | Road (car/bus) | 2 to 3 hours (traffic dependent) |
| Meerut to Delhi | Old rail routes | 1.5 to 2+ hours |

The time savings are significant. If you travel daily, you get back one to two hours every single day.

## **What Is the Distance from Begumpul to Delhi?**

The full corridor is 82.15 km. Begumpul is not at the very start of the corridor (Modipuram is the northernmost station), so the distance from Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan is somewhat shorter, roughly 70 to 75 km by rail alignment.

By road, the distance between Begumpul and central Delhi landmarks is approximately 65 to 75 km, depending on the route you take, but road distance does not account for time lost in traffic, diversions, or signals.

The RRTS does not follow road routes. It has its own elevated and underground alignment designed for speed and efficiency, which means you cover the distance much faster than any road vehicle can.

If someone asks you, "How far is Begumpul from Delhi?" the honest answer is: about 70 to 75 km by rail, and you can cover that distance in roughly 45 to 55 minutes on the RRTS.

## **Ticket Price: How Much Does Begumpul to Delhi Cost?**

The RRTS uses a distance-based fare system. This means the longer your journey, the higher the fare. There is no single fixed price for all passengers.

Here is what you need to know about the fare structure:

**Standard Class:** This is the regular coach. Fares start at around Rs. 20 for very short distances and go up based on how far you travel. For the full corridor or longer distances, expect fares in the range of Rs. 100 to Rs. 150 or more.

**Premium Class:** Some RRTS trains have a premium coach with wider seats and more comfort. The premium fare is higher than standard class for the same distance.

For the specific Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan route, fares are calculated based on the actual distance between those two stations. You will see the exact fare when you check the app or the station ticket machine.

**How to pay:**

- **App (RapidX App):** Download the official RRTS app, add money to your account, and generate a QR code ticket before travel.
- **QR code ticket:** Available through the app and at some ticket machines. Very fast and convenient.
- **Station ticket vending machine:** Available at every station. You can buy a single journey ticket by selecting your destination and paying by cash or card.
- **Contactless smart card:** You can use a travel card for tap-in, tap-out travel. This is the best option for daily commuters because it is fast and often comes with convenience benefits.
- **Counter tickets:** If you are not comfortable with machines, a staffed counter is available at stations.

**Tip for daily commuters:** Use the travel card or the app. Buying a ticket every day from the machine takes extra time during peak hours. Loading your card once and tapping in and out is much faster.

## **How to Travel from Begumpul to Delhi: Step by Step for First-Timers**

If this is your first time using the RRTS, here is exactly what to do:

**Step 1: Plan before you leave home.** Check the train schedule using the RapidX app or the official NCRTC website. Know which train you want to catch and what time it leaves Begumpul.

**Step 2: Buy your ticket in advance if possible.** You can buy a QR code ticket on the app before you even leave home. This saves time at the station. If you prefer, buy at the station machine when you arrive.

**Step 3: Reach the station a few minutes early.** There is a security check at the entry gate, similar to an airport or metro station. Your bags may go through a scanner, and you may walk through a metal detector. Arriving 10 to 15 minutes early is a good habit, especially during peak hours.

**Step 4: Go through the entry gate.** Tap your card, scan your QR code, or show your ticket at the gate. The gates will open, and you walk through.

**Step 5: Find the correct platform.** Look at the digital displays at the station. They show which platform the next train to Delhi is departing from and how many minutes until the train arrives.

**Step 6: Wait on the platform and board safely.** When the train arrives, wait for passengers to exit first. Then board calmly. Do not rush. There is always another train in a few minutes.

**Step 7: Check your destination on the train display.** Inside the train, digital displays show the upcoming stations. Keep an eye on these so you know when to get off.

**Step 8: Exit at Sarai Kale Khan (or your chosen Delhi station).** When you reach your station, tap out at the exit gate. If you are using a QR code, scan it to exit.

**Step 9: Connect to your final destination.** At Sarai Kale Khan, you can connect to the Delhi Metro, buses, autos, or cabs. At Anand Vihar, you can connect to the metro, bus terminal, and Anand Vihar Railway Station.

That is it. The whole process is smooth and easy once you have done it once.

## **What Does the Ride Actually Feel Like?**

This section is for people who have never been on a high-speed rail and want to know what to expect.

The first thing you notice is how quiet the train is compared to a road journey. There is almost no noise, no honking, and no bumps. The train glides on the track.

The seats are comfortable and forward-facing in standard class. There is air conditioning throughout. The coaches are clean and well-maintained. There are overhead luggage racks, charging points at some seats, and digital displays inside showing station names and upcoming stops.

When the train reaches full speed, you will notice the scenery moving fast outside the window. At 160 km/h, the ride is smooth but you will feel the speed when you look outside. It is a noticeably different experience from a regular train or bus.

The coaches are not usually overcrowded because the system is designed for high capacity and frequent services. During peak hours it can get busier, but it is nothing like a packed local train.

The overall experience is calm, efficient, and comfortable. Most commuters use the journey to read, check their phone, or simply rest. By the time you reach Delhi, you feel far less tired than you would after sitting in traffic for two hours.

## **Who Should Use This Route?**

The Begumpul to Delhi RRTS route is most useful for:

**Office commuters:** If you work in Delhi and live in Meerut, this is the best commute option available. You get a fixed travel time, no traffic stress, and a comfortable journey both ways.

**Students:** If you study in Delhi or Noida and live near Begumpul, the fare is very affordable and the travel time is predictable. You can plan your college schedule around train timings without worrying about road delays.

**Business travellers:** If you have regular meetings in Delhi and need to be on time, the RRTS gives you a reliable arrival time. You cannot control road traffic. You can control which train you take.

**Families visiting Delhi:** If you are visiting Delhi for shopping, medical appointments, or tourism and do not want to drive, the RRTS is a comfortable and affordable option.

**Senior citizens and differently-abled passengers:** RRTS stations are designed with accessibility in mind. Lifts and escalators are available. If you find driving or bus travel difficult, the RRTS is a genuinely easier option.

**First-time travellers from Meerut:** If you have never tried the RRTS and want to see what it is like, just take it once. You will almost certainly prefer it to road travel.

## **Station Details: What You Will Find at Begumpul Station**

Begumpul station, like all RRTS stations, is built to a high standard. Here is what you will find there:

- **Entry and exit gates** with QR code and card scanners
- **Security check** area with bag scanners and body check
- **Ticket vending machines** where you can buy single journey or return tickets
- **Ticket counter** for passengers who need assistance
- **Platform displays** showing train timings, destination, and minutes until arrival
- **Waiting area** on the platform with seating
- **Escalators and stairs** to reach the elevated platform
- **Lifts** for accessibility
- **Digital information screens** throughout the station

The station is clean, well-lit, and managed. There is staff available during operating hours. If you are confused, you can ask station staff and they will guide you.

## **Delhi Side: What to Do After You Arrive**

When you arrive at Sarai Kale Khan, you are at the Delhi end of the RRTS corridor. Here is what you can do from there:

**Sarai Kale Khan (main Delhi RRTS terminal):**

- Connect to the Delhi Metro at the nearby Hazrat Nizamuddin or Indraprastha metro stations
- Take a bus from the ISBT Sarai Kale Khan bus terminal, one of Delhi's largest bus terminals
- Book an auto or cab for local travel
- Access Hazrat Nizamuddin Railway Station for onward train travel

**Anand Vihar (if you exit there instead):**

- Connect to the Anand Vihar Metro Station on the Blue Line and Pink Line
- Access Anand Vihar ISBT for buses to eastern UP and other destinations
- Access Anand Vihar Railway Terminal for trains

**New Ashok Nagar:**

- Connect to the Blue Line Delhi Metro

The connectivity at these Delhi stations is very strong. Once you step off the RRTS, you have multiple options to reach any part of Delhi or beyond.

**Practical tip:** Before you travel, decide which Delhi station you want to exit from based on your final destination. If you are going toward Connaught Place or central Delhi, getting off at Anand Vihar and taking the metro may be faster. If you are going toward south Delhi or need a bus, Sarai Kale Khan works better.

## **Route Map: Visual Guide to the Corridor**

Imagine a straight line running from Modipuram in northern Meerut down through the city, past Ghaziabad, and into Delhi ending at Sarai Kale Khan. That is roughly the shape of the RRTS corridor.

Here is a simplified text version of the corridor showing where Begumpul sits:

MODIPURAM

    ↓

SHATABDI NAGAR

    ↓

Begumpul sits at the third station from the Meerut end. This means when you board at Begumpul going toward Delhi, you still have a large portion of the corridor ahead of you — which is good because you will reach Delhi without any stop at Modipuram or Shatabdi Nagar.

## **Travel Comparison: RRTS vs Road vs Other Options**

Many commuters wonder whether the RRTS is really better than driving or taking a bus. Here is a simple comparison:

| **Travel Option** | **Typical Time** | **Cost** | **Comfort** | **Predictability** |
| RRTS (Namo Bharat) | 45 to 55 minutes | Low to medium fare | High (AC, smooth) | Very high (fixed schedule) |
| Private car | 1.5 to 3 hours | Fuel + tolls (high) | Medium | Low (traffic-dependent) |
| Bus (UP Roadways / private) | 2 to 3.5 hours | Low | Low to medium | Low |
| Shared cab/taxi | 1.5 to 2.5 hours | Medium to high | Medium | Low |
| Older train routes | 1.5 to 2+ hours | Low | Low to medium | Medium |

For most daily commuters, the RRTS wins on time, comfort, and predictability. It may not always win on pure cost if you are comparing it to the cheapest bus ticket, but when you factor in time savings and comfort, it is by far the most efficient option.

## **Safety and Comfort Tips for the Ride**

Here are some practical tips to make your RRTS journey easier, especially in the first few weeks:

**Arrive early at the station.** The security check takes a few minutes. If you arrive just as your train is due, you may miss it. Give yourself at least 10 minutes.

**Keep your ticket ready.** Have your QR code or card ready before you reach the gate. Do not search for it in a queue.

**Travel light when possible.** You can carry a bag, but extremely large luggage may be inconvenient on a commuter train. If you are travelling with large luggage, go a little early so you are not rushing.

**Stand in the right area on the platform.** Station markings show where different coaches stop. If you want to board a specific coach (like the premium coach or the women's coach), stand in the right zone before the train arrives.

**Do not rush when the train arrives.** The train waits at the station for a fixed time. Let people exit first, then board calmly.

**Use the app for real-time train schedules.** The RapidX app shows live schedules, train positions, and fare information. It is the best tool for daily commuters.

**Keep your valuables secure.** The RRTS is a safe system, but as with any public transport, keep your phone and wallet in secure pockets.

**Be respectful of quiet zones and priority seating.** Priority seats are marked for senior citizens, differently-abled passengers, and pregnant women. Respect these.

## **The Begumpul Route for Residents: What It Means Locally**

If you live near Begumpul, this train station has probably changed how you think about going to Delhi.

Before the RRTS, a trip to Delhi meant planning around traffic. You left home an hour earlier to account for jams. You came back exhausted. You might cancel plans if it was going to rain because road conditions become terrible.

Now, you can leave Begumpul in the morning, reach Delhi in under an hour, finish your work or meetings, and be back in Meerut by evening without being worn out. That changes your daily routine in a meaningful way.

For students, it means you can take up Delhi-based colleges or coaching centers without needing to shift accommodation. For office workers, it means a Delhi-based job is now a realistic daily commute option. For families, it means visiting relatives or seeing a doctor in Delhi does not require an overnight trip.

The RRTS is not just a train. For Meerut residents, it is access to Delhi — fast, affordable, and reliable.

## **First-Time Rider Checklist**

Before your first trip from Begumpul to Delhi, run through this list:

- Download the RapidX app on your phone
- Check the train schedule and plan which train to take
- Buy your ticket in advance (app or station machine)
- Reach the station 10 to 15 minutes before your train
- Keep a valid photo ID with you
- Know which Delhi station you want to exit at
- Plan your onward travel from the Delhi station (metro, auto, bus)
- Keep your phone charged (you will need it for the QR code if using app tickets)
- Travel light — a backpack or handbag is fine
- Stand on the correct side of the platform for your train direction

## **A Note on Meerut City Metro Stations**

The RRTS corridor in Meerut includes some stations that are designed for local Meerut city travel, in addition to the intercity RRTS trains. This is sometimes confusing for new riders.

In simple terms: some Meerut stations handle both RRTS intercity trains and local metro-style services. This makes the system useful both for going to Delhi and for moving around within Meerut.

Begumpul serves as a key station for both purposes. You can use it to travel to Delhi on the full-corridor RRTS, or for shorter trips within the Meerut section.

The displays at the station will clearly show which trains are stopping and where they are going. Always check the display before boarding to make sure you are getting on the right train.

## **Final Thoughts: Is the RRTS Worth It for Begumpul Commuters?**

If you live near Begumpul and you need to reach Delhi regularly, the answer is yes. The RRTS is worth it.

It is not the cheapest option if you compare just the raw ticket price to the most basic bus fare. But when you factor in time, energy, reliability, and comfort, the RRTS gives you something no road journey can match — predictability. You know when you leave, and you know when you arrive.

For daily commuters, that predictability alone is worth a great deal. It means better sleep schedules, less stress, more productive mornings, and evenings where you actually have energy left after the journey.

The Begumpul to Delhi RRTS corridor is one of the best transit investments in the Delhi-NCR region. If you have not tried it yet, your first ride will probably convince you.

## **Common Questions: Begumpul to Delhi FAQ**

**Is Begumpul to Delhi metro or RRTS?** It is RRTS, also called Namo Bharat. This is not the Delhi Metro. The RRTS is a separate high-speed intercity rail system. You can connect to the Delhi Metro once you reach Delhi-side RRTS stations.

**What is the distance from Begumpul to Delhi?** The full RRTS corridor is 82.15 km. The distance from Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan (main Delhi station) is approximately 70 to 75 km by the rail alignment.

**How much time does it take from Begumpul to Delhi?** Approximately 45 to 55 minutes from Begumpul to Sarai Kale Khan, depending on the service type. The full corridor from Modipuram to Sarai Kale Khan takes 55 to 58 minutes.

**What is the ticket price from Begumpul to Delhi?** Fares are distance-based starting from around Rs. 20. For longer distances like the near-full corridor, expect fares in the range of Rs. 100 to Rs. 150 or more in standard class. Check the app or station machine for the exact current fare.

**What are the station names on this route?** Starting from Meerut: Modipuram, Shatabdi Nagar, Begumpul, Meerut Central, Bhaisali, Meerut South, Modinagar North, Modinagar South, Muradnagar, Duhai, Guldhar, Ghaziabad, Sahibabad, Anand Vihar, New Ashok Nagar, Sarai Kale Khan.

**Is this route good for daily travel?** Yes. The RRTS is specifically designed for daily commuters. It is fast, frequent, air-conditioned, and affordable. Most regular commuters find it far better than road travel.

**Which is the main Delhi station for this route?** Sarai Kale Khan is the main Delhi-end terminal of the corridor. It has connections to buses, metro, and rail. Anand Vihar is also a major interchange station on the Delhi side.

**Can I use one ticket for more than one service?** The RRTS ticket covers your RRTS journey. For connecting to the Delhi Metro or buses, you will need a separate ticket for those services. Integration of ticketing across systems is improving, so check the latest information on the RapidX app.

**Is there a premium class option?** Yes. Some RRTS trains offer a premium coach with wider and more comfortable seats. Premium class fares are higher than standard class fares.

**Can senior citizens and differently-abled passengers use this easily?** Yes. RRTS stations have lifts, escalators, and designated facilities for accessibility. Priority seating is available inside the train.

**Where do I buy a ticket?** You can buy tickets through the RapidX app (recommended), at station vending machines, or at a staffed ticket counter at the station.

**What is the best time to travel to avoid crowds?** If you want a quieter journey, avoid peak office hours (roughly 8 to 10 AM toward Delhi, and 6 to 8 PM return). Midday journeys are usually less crowded.

**Is there parking near Begumpul station?** Limited parking is available near the station. Two-wheelers can be parked more easily. For four-wheelers, check local parking availability or consider using an auto or e-rickshaw to reach the station.
